era: World/Indie. E=5 -- cross-cultural fusion, polyrhythmic, structural hybridity. Self-released. v1.49.352.

location.formative: Seattle cross-cultural scene. Somali diaspora in Rainier Valley. Indie infrastructure + immigrant communities.
location.career: Iska Dhaaf. 2013-2016. Self-released. Nathan Quiroga + Nic Ruczinski.

members.name: Nathan Quiroga
members.note: From Curtains for You. Cross-cultural collaboration.

theory_nodes.concept: World/Indie genre enters the 360 -- structural hybridity, not tourism. The engine recognizes Seattle's music is not monolithically white/rock/English.
examples: Iska Dhaaf: Somali name, East African rhythms, indie rock texture.
The '/' marks a bridge, not a blend.
theory_nodes.description: World/Indie genre enters. Structural hybridity. The diversity is load-bearing.

genealogy.relationship: Iska Dhaaf at 211. World/Indie genre milestone.

theory_nodes.concept: Collaboration vs. appropriation -- authenticity through partnership, not sampling. Bridge with traffic both ways. People from multiple traditions building together.
examples: Quiroga + Ruczinski: different backgrounds, shared project.
The bridge had traffic going both ways.
theory_nodes.description: Collaboration vs. appropriation. Partnership as authenticity model.

cross_references.relationship: Previous degree. Merge (210) to self-released (211). Solo to collaboration.
Same E=5. Self-released. Atmospheric (209) vs. polyrhythmic (211). Same energy, different textures.
Next degree. World/indie (211) to dream pop (212). Cross-genre to single-genre.
